rulings.Case Title: LISA CASTILLO ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment or, In the Alternative, summary Adjudication is DENIED. The Court did not consider Defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) As to Plaintiff's design defect claims, Defendant failed to demonstrate that it supplied a specific product to the Navy pursuant to a contract and specific MIL-SPEC that required asbestos between January 1980 through October 14, 1982, as not all packing MIL-SEPCS specified asbestos as a required ingredient. (Springs Declaration, Ex. H [MIL-P-17303C].) As to Plaintiff's failure to warn claims, Defendant failed to demonstrate that it complied with the Navy's warning requirements between 1980 and 1982. Captain Margaret McCloskey's declaration is negated by evidence from Plaintiff demonstrating that MIL-STD-129G was superseded by MIL-STD-129H on January 3, 1978, amended on July 1, 1980. (Rose Declaration, Ex. 12.) Defendant's alternative failure to warn theory that no warning would have reached Plaintiff or changed his use of packing products is speculative. As to Plaintiff's claims of exposure at Butcher Boys, Defendants failed to demonstrate that it conducted comprehensive discovery to determine Plaintiff's evidence on this issue. (See Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1440.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Plaintiff Gordon Andersen S Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant California Drywall Company's Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff Gordon Andersen's Claim for Punitive Damages is GRANTED. The Court did not consider Defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses demonstrate that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Exs. D, F to Kott Declaration [Plaintiff's responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 24-27 including Exhibit A to response No. 25].) Plaintiff's objection to paragraph 3 of the Kott Declaration regarding Plaintiff's response in Exhibit A is overruled. Plaintiff failed to submit sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The deposition of Stephen Eckstrom and declaration of Richard Cohen, M.D., M.P.H. are not sufficient to create a triable issue as they do not demonstrate a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Exs. B and D to Tashjian Declaration.) While Plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1049; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 60-61.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
